title: Non-cancerous (benign) brain tumours - NHS
text:

Information: 
Find out more The Brain Tumour Charity: living with a brain tumour 
Causes of non-cancerous brain tumours 
It's not known what causes most non-cancerous brain tumours.
But there are some things that can increase your risk, including:
your age – brain tumours are most common in older adults (but they can affect young adults and children too) having close relatives who've had brain tumours having had radiotherapy to your head before (for example, if you had a head or neck cancer as a child) having a rare genetic condition that causes tumours, such as neurofibromatosis or tuberous sclerosis 
Information: 
Find out more Cancer Research UK: risks and causes of brain tumours 
Help and support if you have a brain tumour 
If you have a brain tumour, your care team will support you throughout your treatment. They can give you information about local support services you may find helpful.
There are also national cancer charities that can offer support and information.
The Brain Tumour Charity Information and support for anyone affected by brain tumours.
Website: www.thebraintumourcharity.org Helpline: 0808 800 0004 Email: support@thebraintumourcharity.org The Brain Tumour Charity: online support groups The Brain Tumour Charity: local support groups The Brain Tumour Charity:
